data.title: Australia reports largest trade surplus in eight months for Oct, driven by rebound in exports
data.description:
• Australia's trade surplus on goods increased to AUD 5.95 billion in October 2024, up from a downwardly revised AUD 4.53 billion in September, surpassing market expectations for a gain of AUD 4.55 billion.
  • This was the biggest trade surplus since February, largely driven by a recovery in exports.
  • Exports of goods from Australia grew by 3.6% from a month earlier to AUD 42.15 billion in October 2024, shifting from a revised 4.7% slump in the previous month.
  • Imports of goods to Australia edged up 0.1% month-over-month to AUD 36.20 billion in October 2024, compared with a downwardly revised 2.8% decline in the previous month.
  • The S&P/ASX 200 Index rose by 0.2% to around 8,480 on Thursday, recouping losses from the previous session. The Australian dollar stabilized around $0.643 on Thursday after data revealed that Australia posted its largest trade surplus in eight months for October, driven by a recovery in exports.

  • ETFs: (EWA), (FXA), (FLAU).

  • More on Australia

    • Australia's economy grows at weaker-than-expected pace in Q3; services activity remains in expansion
    • Australia's retail sales rise more than expected in October by 0.6% M/M
    • RBA Minutes: Discussed scenarios that could warrant rate cuts; retains restrictive monetary policy
    • Australia's trade surplus hits 4-month high despite slight declines in both exports and imports
    • Australia's monthly CPI indicator fell to three-year low of 2.7% in August

data.title: Disney hikes cash dividend by 33% to $1.00/share
data.description:
• The Walt Disney (NYSE:DIS) declared a cash dividend of $1.00 per share. This represents a 33% increase over the $0.75 per share paid to shareholders during fiscal year 2024.
  • The dividend will be paid in two installments of $0.50/share semi-annually, vs. prior semi-annual dividend of $0.45.
  • Payable Jan. 16; to shareholders of record Dec. 16; ex-div Dec. 16.
  • Payable Jul. 23; to shareholders of record Jun. 24; ex-div Jun. 24.
  • See DIS Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Thursday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
7:30 AM Challenger Job-Cut Report

This monthly report counts and categorizes announcements of corporate layoffs based on mass layoff data from state departments of labor 49K job cuts are expected in November after US employers announced 55,597 job cuts in October 2024.

8:30 AM Initial Jobless Claims

Initial Jobless Claims measures the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time during the past week. The jobless claims is expected to edge higher to 215K after holding steady at 213K.

8:30 AM International Trade in Goods and Services

Trade in goods and services between U.S. residents and residents of other countries each month. U.S. sales are exports and U.S. purchases are imports. The difference between the exports and imports is the trade balance. The trade balance deficit is expected to narrow to $75.4B in October after widening to $84.40B in September.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report

The Energy Information Administration Natural Gas Storage report measures the change in the number of cubic feet of natural gas held in underground storage over the prior week. US utilities withdraw 2 billion cubic feet of gas into storage during the week that ended November 27, 2024.

11:00 AM Treasury Buyback Announcement

Buybacks are a cash management tool to give the government flexibility to manage the public debt.

12:15 PM Fed’s Barkin Speech

Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin to speak before in-person Charlotte Regional Business Alliance 2024 Economic Outlook event.

2:00 PM Treasury Buyback Results

Institutions that are approved by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York (FRBNY) to conduct open market transactions may participate in a buyback. FRBNY accepts offers with the best value relative to prevailing market prices. Shortly after the operation, Treasury publishes summary results.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed balance sheet is a weekly report listing the assets and liabilities of the Federal Reserve System. Click here to see the weekly economic calendar.

Click here to see the weekly economic calendar.

data.title: Bitcoin crosses $100,000 as Trump picks crypto backer, Paul Atkins for SEC chair, Powell comments
data.description:
• The Bitcoin price has finally managed to cross $100,000 milestone mark as the cryptocurrency continues to see a meteoric rise after Donald Trump's win in the U.S. presidential election. The world's oldest cryptocurrency has risen ~45% since the U.S. Election Day.
  • The Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is trading 7.7% higher at $103,195 after it reversed from 99,849.99 on Nov. 22, just under $100,000 landmark figure.
  • The crypto rally was further fueled by the nomination of cryptocurrency advocate Paul Atkins to chair the Securities and Exchange Commission. “He believes in the promise of robust, innovative capital markets that are responsive to the needs of Investors, & that provide capital to make our Economy the best in the World. He also recognizes that digital assets & other innovations are crucial to Making America Greater than Ever Before,” Trump wrote on Truth Social.
  • Paul Atkins will replace Gary Gensler, who has been leading the U.S. government’s crackdown on the crypto industry, during the Biden regime. Gensler announced last month that he would be stepping down from his post on the day that Trump is inaugurated — Jan. 20, 2025.
  • Earlier today, Powell commented on bitcoin, stating that “People use bitcoin as a speculative asset — it’s like gold. It’s just like gold, only it’s virtual, it’s digital. People are not using it as a form of payment or as a store of value. It’s highly volatile. It's not a competitor for the dollar, it's really a competitor for gold.”
  • Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is becoming one of the most prominent "Trump trades" in the aftermath of the 2024 election. Back on the campaign trail, now President-elect Donald Trump jumped on the crypto bandwagon, with promises to create a Bitcoin Strategic Reserve, fight CBDCs, and defend the right to self-custody.

data.title: Meta to build $10B AI data center in Louisiana, to work with Entergy for energy needs
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) said Wednesday it plans to spend $10B to build a 4M sq ft artificial intelligence data center in Louisiana, in what would be the company's largest data center in the world.

To power the data center - which at its largest point will extend more than a mile from front to back - utility company Entergy (ETR) will add clean power plants to its system to meet growing power demands, including from the data center.

Meta (META) said it will match its electricity use with 100% clean and renewable energy, and will work with Entergy (ETR) to bring at least 1,500 MW of new renewable energy to the grid.

Meta (META) said earlier this week it is requesting proposals from nuclear energy developers in an attempt to harness nuclear energy for further AI data centers.

Entergy (ETR), which has two nuclear power plants in Louisiana, previously received legislative approval for investment in transmission and generation to serve Amazon's upcoming cloud services facility in Mississippi.

data.title: North Carolina city sues Duke Energy over climate change
data.description:
The town of Carrboro, North Carolina, said Wednesday it has filed a lawsuit against Duke Energy (NYSE:DUK), claiming the utility has deceived its customers and the broader public about the environmental dangers caused by fossil fuels.

Carrboro said it is "on the hook" for millions of dollars in road repairs, rising energy bills and other infrastructure costs to mitigate the harmful effects of climate change.

The lawsuit claimed Duke Energy's (DUK) top executives have increased reliance on coal and gas for electricity despite being aware of the risks from fossil fuels for more than 50 years.

Carrboro officials want Duke Energy (DUK) to compensate the town and residents for the costs they say they were forced to pay as a result of the utility's alleged coverup.

data.title: Ahead of OPEC+ output decision, Saudi Arabia losing grip on global oil markets
data.description:
Crude oil futures finished lower Wednesday as uncertainty over the coming OPEC+ decision on production levels outweighed support from weekly U.S. data showing a larger than expected drop in crude stockpiles.

The Energy Information Administration reported mixed supply and demand data for the week ended Friday, as U.S. commercial crude oil stocks fell by 5.1M barrels while distillate stocks jumped 3.4M barrels and gasoline inventories rose 2.4M barrels.

OPEC+ is set to meet tomorrow after postponing a gathering that had been set for December 1, and the group is expected to again delay its plan to begin unwinding production cuts due to softness in the crude market and fears that an increase in output would add to a surplus.

But "as this voluntary decline in output wears on month after month, increasing discord within OPEC appears to be developing that could place Saudi Arabia in a position to either independently cut production further or join other producers in lifting output in order to maintain some semblance of market share," Ritterbusch analysts said.

Meanwhile, the U.S. Treasury Department announced sanctions on 35 entities and vessels it said play a critical role in transporting petroleum from Iran to foreign markets.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for January delivery ended -2% to $68.54/bbl, and front-month February Brent crude (CO1:COM) finished -1.8% to $72.31/bbl.

U.S. natural gas futures (NG1:COM) were little changed ahead of the EIA's weekly inventory report, with the front-month January contract closing at $3.043/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Saudi Arabia's sway over OPEC long meant unquestioned dominance of the global oil market, "but those days are over, at least for now," The Wall Street Journal reports.

Saudi is struggling to execute its plan to keep prices elevated, as the cartel's increasingly fractious members are pushing to pump more and maximize short-term profits, in part due to the expectation of growing competition from U.S. shale drillers emboldened by Donald Trump's re-election.

The kingdom looks likely to keep the spigots tight on its own production, but the United Arab Emirates has been allowed to add more barrels into the market from January, and Iraq and Kazakhstan also want to bring more production of their own, which would boost supplies further and likely depress prices.

data.title: F.N.B. Corporation prices $500M senior notes offering
data.description:
• F.N.B. Corporation (NYSE:FNB) announced the pricing of its offering of $500M aggregate principal amount of its fixed rate / floating rate senior notes due 2030.
  • The notes will bear interest at 5.722% per annum, payable semi-annually in arrears on June 11 and December 11, of each year, commencing on June 11, 2025, and ending on December 11, 2029.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 11, 2024.
  • The company expects to use the net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, which may include investments at the holding company level, capital to support the growth of the company's banking subsidiary and refinancing of outstanding indebtedness.
  • Source: Press Release
